summary: South Korea's transition is led by the Ministry of Science and ICT, which is establishing a national post-quantum cryptography transition infrastructure including a national PQC body, with KISA running annual sector-by-sector pilot transition programmes for participating companies. Korean guidance applies both the NIST post-quantum standards and domestic Korean post-quantum algorithms.
govActors:
MSIT | Ministry of Science and ICT, establishing the national post-quantum cryptography transition infrastructure and a national PQC body, with budget allocated for the transition
KISA | Korea Internet & Security Agency, reference guidance on post-quantum cryptography and the annual sector-by-sector PQC pilot transition support programmes
migrationTimeline:
2025 | Sector-by-sector PQC pilot transition support programme (energy, medical, public administration)
2026 | PQC pilot transition project extended to telecom, finance, transport, defence and space sectors
provenance: summary + govActors (MSIT): MSIT national PQC transition infrastructure row and the 2026 KRW 8.12 trillion budget row
govActors (KISA): KISA PQC reference page and the 2025 and 2026 pilot transition call rows

migrationTimeline 2025/2026: the 2025 (energy, medical, public administration) and 2026 (telecom, finance, transport, defence, space) pilot transition rows. These are programme phases rather than a national completion timeline

dominantProcess Mixed: a KISA included candidate description applies post-quantum cryptography labelled NIST PQC/KPQC, and a separate KISA briefing references the NIST PQC effort, while MSIT pursues national/sovereign PQC infrastructure. Korea also runs its own KpqC national algorithm competition, but the dedicated KpqC Research Group documents are included:false and not relied on. Mixed assigned because both the NIST camp and a domestic Korean scheme are attested in included material; a Sovereign reading is plausible if the KpqC scheme is confirmed - REVIEW
targetCompletion: OMITTED, no national completion date stated in the included summaries (a national master plan is referenced only in an included:false KpqC notice)
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED, no specific FIPS or algorithm names stated in the included results summaries
Model 2026-06-14 (NIST-bloc / contextualiser, Medium): Runs the KpqC national competition but keeps algorithms NIST-interoperable; on the sovereign boundary. Reclassified from Mixed.

mainRegulation:
KCMVP cryptographic module validation (government) | national | binding-by-market-access
KpqC national algorithm selections (2025: NTRU+, SMAUG-T, AIMer, HAETAE) | national | guidance
MSIT national PQC transition programme (2025-2035) | national | guidance
obligation: Government cryptographic modules must pass KCMVP validation, which selects the national KpqC algorithms; the MSIT programme runs phased sector pilots to 2035. There is no economy-wide statutory mandate.
